vitals.terrain: coastal roads with continuous ascents and descents, late-race climbs
vitals.aid_stations: 6+ (Aikawa 19km, Nyuzaki 42km, Washizaki 75km, Ryotsu 103km, Tada 138km, Ogi 163km, Sobama 184km)
vitals.participation_trend: stable, one of Japan's largest cycling events

course_description.overview: 210 km clockwise loop circumnavigating Sado Island starting and finishing at Kawaharada Elementary School grounds in Sawada. Features stunning northern coastline (Osado) in the first half, crossing Kuninaka Plain, with brutal late-race climbing.

key_segments.name: Z-zaka (Hanezaka) Climb
key_segments.description: Steep climb up a sheer cliff along northern coastline, 130 m elevation gain.

key_segments.name: Osado Coastline
key_segments.description: Scenic northern coastal roads with views of Sea of Japan and Onogame rock landmark, passing Aikawa, Nyuzaki, Washizaki aid stations.

key_segments.name: Ryotsu Aid Station (halfway)
key_segments.description: Midpoint at noon target, lunch stop before central plain and return.

key_segments.name: Late-race climbs (Tada, Ogi, Sobama)
key_segments.description: Continuous ascents and descents with cutoffs: Tada 13:45, Ogi 15:00, Sobama 16:30.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Highly challenging with relentless up-and-down coastal roads, steep climbs like Z-zaka, and brutal mountainous sections in the second half requiring strong pacing to meet cutoffs.

logistics.getting_there: Ferry from Niigata Port to Ryotsu or Ogi Port on Sado Island; event venue at Kawaharada Elementary School, Sawada (west side). C course finishes at Ryotsu for convenient ferry return.
logistics.accommodation: Available on Sado Island; bib pickup day before (May 16, 12-18:00) at venue.
logistics.registration_details: Online via Sports Entry, Jan 1 - Mar 31, 2026. A Course: 12,000 yen (general), requires prior experience or bike shop check. Includes sacoche bag, EXPO voucher, insurance. Bibs mailed mid-May, pickup May 16.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road-legal bikes only (no tandems, DH bars); mandatory helmet, front/rear lights, bell, brakes. Bike shop inspection required pre-event.
logistics.local_considerations: Rain or shine; obey traffic laws, left-side riding, no side-by-side. Local support with delicacies at aid stations. Ferry logistics key for island access.

final_verdict.one_liner: Japan's premier island road epic: breathtaking coasts meet savage climbs.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced endurance road cyclists seeking iconic scenery, massive fields, and a true sufferfest.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the notable coastal beauty, local hospitality, and prestige of conquering Sado.
pro_tips: Target Ryotsu AS by noon to buffer late cutoffs.
Train for repeated 5-10% coastal pitches and final mountains.
Book early ferry/accommodation; bring tunnel lights.
Sample Sado delicacies at AS for morale boost.
